content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95814 • • • 1015 L STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO 95814

Technical Assistance • • • Administration • • • Lobbyist Legal • • • Enforcement • • • Conflict of Interest
916-322-5662        322-5660              322-5491              322-6441            322-6444


November 26, 1979


Mr. Kirk Alan Pessner
DOBBS & Nielsen
Suite 2560, The Alcoa Building
One Maritime Plaza
San Francisco, California  94111

Dear Mr. Pessner:

I have discussed with other members of the staff the three questions
you raise in your letter of November 13, 1979. We have agreed that
the answer to all three should be "yes."

A person who contributes $5,000 to a ballot measure qualification
committee in year 1, as in your first question, becomes a committee
within the meaning of Government Code Section 82013 and must file as
such under Government Code Section 84204 even if the time of filing
will not be determined until year 2.

Contributions to ballot measure committees do count in the year in
which they are made in determining whether a person has reached
the threshold to qualify as a major donor. As long as the contribution is to or at the behest of a candidate or committee as in
Government Code Section 82013(c), it does not matter that the measure
whose qualification the recipient is supporting or opposing will not
qualify or fail to qualify until the next calendar year.

As for your third question, if a person is a major donor, then by
Government Code Section 84206, 84200 or 84401 campaign statements
must be filed and must contain all of the information required by
Government Code Section 84210. This information includes the total
amount of expenditures made during the period covered by the statement and the itemized data about any person to whom expenditures
of $100 or more have been made during the period.

I hope these remarks satisfactorily answer the questions you have
raised. If I can be of further assistance, you may call me.

Sincerely,

[signature]
JOHN GIANSELLO
Political Reform Analyst

DOBBS & NIELSEN
Attorneys and Counselors at Law

SUITE 2500, THE ALCOA BUILDING
ONE MARITIME PLAZA
SAN FRANCISCO, CA 94111

TELEPHONE (415) 362-1940

November 13, 1979


Mr. John Giansello
Fair Political Practices Commission
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95807

Re: Contributions to qualify an initiative

Dear John:

     This letter will reiterate my questions regarding
reporting of contributions to qualify a measure, and, after
you have discussed this matter with the staff, respond to
me in writing.

     Question No. 1: Does a "person," as defined,
become a major donor committee by contributing $5,000 or
more in 1979 toward the qualification of a measure when it
will not be determined that the measure has qualified or
failed to qualify until 1980?

     Question No. 2: Does a person who has made contri-
butions to California State and local candidates and ballot
measures, but has not reached major donor threshold, become a
major donor committee by contributing to the qualification of
a measure (which will not qualify or fail to qualify until 1980)
when the contributions to candidates and ballot measures plus
the contribution toward the qualification are $5,000 or more?

     Question No. 3: If a person is a major donor in 1979,
must that person list a contribution toward the qualification of
a measure that will not qualify or fail to qualify until 1980?

     If you have any questions on this issue, please do
not hesitate to call me.

     Kind regards,

                                        Very truly yours,


                                        Kirk Alan Pessner
                                        Paralegal

KAP:bc

SACRAMENTO OFFICE · SUITE 250, 1223 EIGHTH STREET, SACRAMENTO, CA 95814 · (916) 446-6752
LOS ANGELES OFFICE · SUITE 309, 812 SOUTH FLOWER STREET, LOS ANGELES, CA 90017 · (213) 626-2867

Question No. 3: If a person is a major donor in 1979, must that person list a contribution toward the qualification of a measure that will not qualify or fail to qualify until 1980?
